subject: PROPOSAL: LCARS component library extraction — reusable across dashboards

content.context: We need to spin off a second LCARS dashboard for a computing-network project. The mesh dashboard components (panels, grids, tables, bars, waveforms, frame) should live in a shared library that any dashboard can import.

lcars-components.description: Shared LCARS Web Components + CSS. No data, no endpoints, no domain logic. Just visual building blocks.
contents: lcars-theme.css — V23.01 Okuda palette, LCARS 2369 named colors, alert level overrides
lcars-base.css — frame geometry, elbow curve (radial gradient), sidebar structure, responsive breakpoints
lcars-components.css — panel tristate, data-grid pills, data-table, bars, dots, chips
core.js — renderNumberGrid, renderAgentSelector, dataWaveformSVG, pushWaveData, delta tracking, alert evaluation
lcars-frame.js — L-shape layout Web Component (sidebar + header + content slots)
lcars-panel.js — tristate collapsible panel Web Component
lcars-data-grid.js — pill grid with semantic grouping (.dg-gap)
lcars-components.distribution: Git submodule for now. npm package later when API stabilizes.

mesh-lcars.description: Fleet dashboard for the safety-quotient mesh. Imports lcars-components as git submodule.
contents: index.html — station panes, subsystem selectors, zone-a containers
js/stations/ — operations.js, science.js, engineering.js, helm.js, tactical.js, medical.js
js/chrome.js — tab switching, theme, subsystem selector
js/data-fetch.js — pulse + status fetch, agentData enrichment
js/init.js — bootstrap, theme restore, subsystem URL restore
mesh-lcars.embedding: mesh-core go:embeds this at build time into meshd binary

computing_network_lcars.description: Dashboard for the computing-network project. Imports same lcars-components submodule.
computing_network_lcars.contents: Own index.html + own station JS modules + own data endpoints
computing_network_lcars.note: Completely independent from mesh-lcars. Shares only the visual component library.

lcars_components_sources: interagent/public/css/inline-legacy.css → split into lcars-theme.css + lcars-base.css + lcars-components.css
interagent/public/css/components.css → merge relevant parts into lcars-components.css
interagent/public/js/core.js → extract generic utilities (renderNumberGrid, dataWaveformSVG, delta, agent selector, alert system)
mesh_lcars_keeps: js/chrome.js, js/data-fetch.js, js/knowledge.js, js/ws-sse.js, js/init.js (mesh-specific data flow)
Station-specific CSS (ohniaka grid, affect grid, engineering grid, etc.)

extraction_plan.go_embed_pattern: meshd builds: go:embed lcars-components/* mesh-lcars/* → static/ directory. Serves at /. Station plugin auto-discovery from /api/stations.
extraction_plan.submodule_pattern: mesh-lcars/components/ → git submodule pointing to lcars-components repo. computing-network-lcars/components/ → same submodule.

content.timing: Extract during mesh-core migration — same coordinated effort. The CSS split represents the hardest part (4300 lines in inline-legacy.css need careful separation of generic vs mesh-specific rules).
content.web_components_later: Current extraction uses plain JS functions + CSS classes. Web Component wrappers (custom elements with shadow DOM) represent a future enhancement — not needed for the submodule approach.
